description_long: This object is a long, gently tapering horn with a wide, open bell at one end and a narrow, closed tip at the other. The surface is smooth and slightly glossy, with a natural, bone-like coloration that transitions from a lighter beige at the bell to a darker brown at the tip. The bell is round and flares gradually, creating a prominent opening for sound projection. There are no visible valves, keys, or mechanical parts along the body, and the entire form is seamless and unadorned. The object appears hollow, as seen from the open bell, and the interior is consistent in color with the exterior. Its overall shape and material suggest it is modeled after a traditional animal horn trumpet, such as an alphorn or shofar, rather than a modern brass trumpet.
description: A long, smooth, horn-shaped trumpet with a wide, flared bell and a natural beige-to-brown coloration. The surface is seamless and slightly glossy, with no visible valves or mouthpiece.
description_view_0: The wide, open bell of the horn is prominently visible, showing the hollow interior and gradual flare.
description_view_1: The side profile reveals the gentle curve and tapering body, with the closed tip and smooth surface.
description_view_2: The bell is seen head-on, emphasizing its round opening and the gradual transition to the narrow body.
description_view_3: The opposite side profile highlights the full length and curvature, with the pointed tip and subtle color gradient.
attribute_bell_flare: The bell is wide and gradually flared, creating a smooth, open mouth.
attribute_valve_configuration: No valves are present along the body; the surface is continuous and unbroken.
attribute_leadpipe_curvature: The leadpipe is straight and seamlessly integrated into the gently tapering body.
attribute_tuning_slide_location: No tuning slide is visible; the body is a single, continuous tube.
